feature.name: Web Authentication Immediate UI mode
feature.creator: kenrb@chromium.org
feature.summary: A new mode for navigator.credentials.get() that causes browser sign-in UI to be displayed to the user if there is a passkey or password for the site that is immediately known to the browser, or else rejects the promise with NotAllowedError if there is no such credential available. This allows the site to avoid showing a sign-in page if the browser can offer a choice of sign-in credentials that are likely to succeed, while still allowing a traditional sign-in page flow for cases where there are no such credentials.

intake.finding: ChromeStatus record (created 2025-03-21 by kenrb@chromium.org). A new mode for navigator.credentials.get() that causes browser sign-in UI to be displayed to the user if there is a passkey or password for the site that is immediately known to the browser, or else rejects the promise with NotAllowedError if there is no such credential available. This allows the site to avoid showing a sign-in page if the browser can offer a choice of sign-in credentials that are likely to succeed, while still allowing a traditional sign-in page flow for cases where there are no such credentials. Category: Miscellaneous. Blink component: Blink>WebAuthentication. Stars: 80.

adoption.finding: The legacy HTML/JS counter 'CredentialsGetImmediateMediationWithWebAuthnOnly' (ChromeStatus timeline, bucket_id 5389) measured 0.0005% of measured Chrome HTTP/HTTPS page loads on 2026-07-20. Tracked since 2025-08-06 with 301 data points. Mapping confidence: declared-name.
